text: Git branching is a powerful feature of version control that helps teams work on code without interfering with each other. A common workflow involves creating a feature branch from the main branch. Developers use this separate branch to build new features or fix bugs. When the work is complete, they submit a pull request to propose merging their changes back into the main branch. This allows team members to review the code, discuss improvements, and ensure quality before the merge. Merging combines the changes from the feature branch with the main codebase. This process keeps the main branch stable while enabling parallel development and smooth collaboration.
